_note: VERIFIED against live logged-in threads.com by performing one real delete, one reply delete, and one undo-repost: item root [data-pressable-container="true"]; menu via div[role="button"]:has(svg[aria-label="More"]); [role="menuitem"] text "Delete"; confirm dialog holds exactly two text-only [role="button"]s ("Delete" / "Cancel") so EXACT text matching is required; repost menu is "Remove"/"Quote" with NO confirm dialog; real time[datetime] timestamps. Threads exposes NO data-testid and its class names are per-build obfuscation, so selectors use only role/aria-label/data-pressable-container. Deleted items stay in the DOM until reload. The /replies route interleaves other authors' parent posts with your replies, so the adapter probes a[href="/@<handle>"] for ownership. STILL UNVERIFIED: dismissControls, and sustained-run behaviour under Meta rate limiting.

postItem.intent: the root container element of a post on a Threads profile feed

replyItem.intent: the root container element of a reply on the Threads profile /replies route

repostItem.intent: the root container element of a reposted thread on the Threads profile /reposts route

menuButton.intent: the "More" (…) button on a post that opens its options menu — an SVG with aria-label "More" inside a clickable div[role="button"]

deleteMenuItem.intent: an item in the open post options menu; the caller text-matches the one whose visible text is "Delete" (the items carry no testid or aria-label, only text)

deleteConfirm.intent: a button in the "Delete post?" confirmation dialog; the dialog holds exactly two such buttons, "Delete" and "Cancel", so the caller text-matches "Delete" exactly — this selector must match BOTH buttons, never be narrowed to one by position

repostButton.intent: the repost button on a post (opens the repost / undo-repost menu) — an SVG with aria-label "Repost" inside a clickable div[role="button"]

undoRepostMenuItem.intent: the "Remove" entry in the repost menu (VERIFIED: the menu holds exactly "Remove" and "Quote"; Remove takes effect with no confirmation dialog)

itemTimestamp.intent: the element inside a post that carries its date — on Threads a real <time> whose datetime attribute holds the ISO timestamp

dismissControls.intent: the close / dismiss control of a modal dialog, popup or toast (UNVERIFIED on Threads)
